scenario_results.reason: Normal itinerary completion used traveler profile, GDS, supplier, payment, fare or rate rule, hotel confirmation, and approval evidence before producing a booking confirmation.
Incomplete traveler identity, itinerary, payment guarantee, and approval prerequisites produced a pending-summary instead of an unsafe hold or confirmation.
Expired fare hold, closed room block, and ineligible negotiated rate were rejected or routed before execution began.
Repricing and rebooking requests that exceeded policy authority were refused and routed to pricing, revenue, supplier-waiver, or approval owners.
Booking, supplier, GDS, hotel guarantee, and payment mismatches stayed blocked and traceable instead of being synthesized into a false final status.
Supplier disruption with linked itinerary conflicts produced a traveler-impact escalation note across air, hotel, and ground bookings.
Identity mismatch, refund reroute, and payment substitution signals stopped booking changes and routed the case to fraud or loss-prevention.
Missing passport, destination, and safety-sensitive prerequisite context triggered pending or routing behavior without legal or safety sufficiency claims.
High-volume disruption intake preserved queue segmentation, traveler impact, evidence readiness, service windows, and authority boundaries.
Room upgrades, resort-fee waivers, luggage recovery, and service compensation were routed to adjacent owners instead of absorbed by the booking lane.
Conflicting DOT, supplier, tenant, and fare-rule sources were recorded as precedence conflicts requiring escalation.
Tenant-specific travel policy, payment-token rules, supplier preferences, and service windows were applied while preserving the public baseline and assumptions.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for the intake-access-and-initiation and retail-consumer-and-hospitality contract.

The package stays inside travel booking intake, prerequisite validation, confirmation readiness, pending summaries, and safe escalation; it does not claim pricing override, refund approval, supplier-waiver decisioning, fraud adjudication, legal travel-document advice, or safety signoff authority.
Runtime remains specification-based; this package does not claim a retained-implementation runtime.
